description_long: This is a long, rectangular cowshed with a low-pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles or tiles. The structure features open eaves along the long sides, providing ventilation and partial visibility into the interior, which is divided into several bays or stalls. The end walls are solid, with one end showing a large, brown panel that likely serves as the main entrance. The base appears to be constructed from concrete or a similar sturdy material, while the upper structure is framed with metal or wood. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and airflow for livestock comfort.
description: A long, rectangular cowshed with a gray gable roof, open eaves, and a solid end wall with a large brown entrance panel. The structure is utilitarian and well-ventilated, suitable for housing livestock.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the cowshed, open eaves, and a glimpse of the interior bays.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the gable roof, solid brown end wall, and partial interior layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with open eaves and visible interior divisions.
description_view_3: End view mirroring view 1, showing the gable roof and solid end wall with interior details.
attribute_door_style: single large wooden or metal sliding barn door
attribute_window_presence: no windows, open eaves for ventilation
